mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized demand expansion across goods and real estate, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when the economy operates below full capacity.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during capacity-constrained periods produce negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold consistently coincide with positive 20-day equity returns across multiple inflation cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 20-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a manufacturing contraction and weakening business investment, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Two-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of historical occurrences.
